title: Infer cheap-stop neutralization regimes from field-synchronized lap shocks
summary: F1 strategy sources stress that VSC/SC periods materially reduce pit-loss and create immediate pit windows.
rationale: F1 strategy sources stress that VSC/SC periods materially reduce pit-loss and create immediate pit windows. Your current features mostly approximate circuit priors, but not race-state shocks where many cars suddenly become viable pit candidates at once.
implementation_hint: Create same-lap field features by Year-Race-LapNumber: median/quantile LapTime_Delta, share of cars with huge positive deltas, variance collapse in positions, and one-lap persistence. Use these to classify green vs likely VSC/SC-like laps, then interact that flag with tyre age, finish margin, and current pit-window features.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on abrupt next-lap pit waves and reduce false negatives when a stop becomes cheap for everyone.
risk: Outlier laps from weather or local incidents may mimic neutralization; validate with leave-race-out folds to avoid overfitting to single events.
